channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-18195] conf-sanity test_61b: mount.lustre: unexpected EA_INODE flag
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for jianyu <yujian@whamcloud.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/26b0b03f-ad2f-4544-9bc6-bd7eeefc3a38

test_61b failed with the following error:

Starting mds1: -o localrecov  /dev/mapper/mds1_flakey /mnt/lustre-mds1
CMD: trevis-39vm6 mkdir -p /mnt/lustre-mds1; mount -t lustre -o localrecov  /dev/mapper/mds1_flakey /mnt/lustre-mds1

Timeout occurred after 369 minutes, last suite running was conf-sanity

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-master/4563 - 5.15.0-88-generic
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-master/4563 - 5.14.0-427.24.1_lustre.el9.x86_64

<<Please provide additional information about the failure here>>

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
conf-sanity test_61b - Timeout occurred after 369 minutes, last suite running was conf-sanity
item.summary: conf-sanity test_61b: mount.lustre: unexpected EA_INODE flag

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56370/
Subject: LU-18195 ldiskfs: support lost+found NO_CHECKS for Ubunutu
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: d306b6fe2f3d376b9dca4d0abb88230ec27b3564

Merged for 2.16. 

James

This ticket is to track the work that has to merge ahead of the 2.16 release being finalized which is only for RHEL 9.4 servers. It's ok to use the same Jira reference to track similar work for other kernels if you wish - Gerrit and Jira are not tightly-coupled in that regard - or else you can open a new Jira ticket to track this work if you prefer.
Please don't close until the other platforms are supported. I already pushed a patch for Ubuntu 22 support.

"James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56370
Subject: LU-18195 ldiskfs: support lost+found NO_CHECKS for Ubunutu
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 407f1d019ac3890672bc6a68d71cc6b9310bf467

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56276/
Subject: LU-18195 osd-ldiskfs: use IGET_NO_CHECKS for objects under lost+found
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 1045c6433c50055c58c1bbdab10a1d447cdb00c2

Andreas,
Since 849a9e6e1 e2fsck: add more checks for ea inode consistency, e2fsck no longer links ea inode under
lost+found. which introduced in 1.47.1.
This should be unlikely to happen if the server has more recent e2fsprogs, but indeed it is a potential problem with older e2fsck.

patch 56276 is not ideal, I checked ext4_lookup() but I don't see how to get a "side" flag into it.
I don't have a better idea to check if we are doing lookup under lost+found other than looking if it's s_first_ino, if lost+found was removed and recreated it could have a different ino.

"Li Dongyang <dongyangli@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56276
Subject: LU-18195 osd-ldiskfs: use IGET_NO_CHECKS for object under lost+found
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 13406b68178beb2bed71bae0f049a76712b9b488

On the one hand, if an EA inode is accessed directly then it means some kind of filesystem corruption is present. On the other hand, one bad inode should not trigger the filesystem to go read-only, especially not in a loop that makes the whole server unusable. While this is unlikely to happen (just a bit flip, or e2fsck linking an orphan inode there?), I'd rather make the code more robust rather than try to avoid this situation.

Some other options might include:

  • unlinking the orphan EA inode from lost+found if it is found there, since it should really be attached to the inode. might be tricky, especially if e2fsck just links it back, unless e2fsck is finding the original inode and relinking it there, but it may have already tried this...
  • pass some "side" flag around lookup_one_len() to ldiskfs to indicate that this is OI Scrub running and it shouldn't complain about this case (which might also simplify the original patch https://review.whamcloud.com/55896 "LU-18080 ldiskfs: add NO_CHECKS flags for ext4_iget()". I thought we already did something similar for OI Scrub in the past because of a similar patch, but I don't recall what the workaround was.

passing in LDISKFS_IGET_NO_CHECKS is not really doable, we need to change lookup_one_len()>__lookup_slow()>ldiskfs_lookup() that's too much changes in vfs.

The more important issue is ext4 will just remount ro when it's not expecting the EA_INODE flag, which seems
excessive but also makes sense since we manually linked the ea inode to lost+found using debugfs.

I think either we should figure out how to do test_61b without making ea_inode directly accessible, since we are really just handling a special case created by linking ea inode to lost+found, or relax the ext4 do not remount ro, then fix the looping in osd_initial_OI_scrub.

dongyang, it looks like some more exceptions need to be added in osd_ios_lf_fill()->osd_lookup_one_len() (and other osd_ios_*_fill() functions in oi_scrub.c) that are calling lookup_one_len()->__ldiskfs_iget() to pass LDISKFS_IGET_NO_CHECKS (if that is even possible, since we don't control the caller), or more gracefully handle the error returned from lookup_one_len() in this case to avoid looping forever.

The MDS debug log shows a continuous stream of failed lookups, but nothing else in the logs to show at what level it is looping:

00100000:00000001:1.0:1723880918.719244:0:696610:0:(osd_scrub.c:1985:do_osd_ios_lf_fill()) Process entered
00100000:10000000:1.0:1723880918.719363:0:696610:0:(osd_scrub.c:1997:do_osd_ios_lf_fill()) lustre-MDT0000: cannot lookup child '<161>': rc = -117
00100000:00000001:1.0:1723880918.719365:0:696610:0:(osd_scrub.c:1999:do_osd_ios_lf_fill()) Process leaving (rc=18446744073709551499 : -117 : ffffffffffffff8b)
00100000:00000001:1.0:1723880918.719367:0:696610:0:(osd_scrub.c:1985:do_osd_ios_lf_fill()) Process entered

Possibly adding a CDEBUG() line in osd_ios_general_scan() that is printing buf.oifb_items and buf.oifb_dentry.d_name and filp->f_pos to see whether it is getting up to that level, or where it is stuck. I can understand that we don't want to return an error to the iterator for ESTALE (it should continue to process other items), but it shouldn't reprocess the same filename multiple times.

Hmm, the servers in the failed test session were running 2.15.65.29 on el9.4, and the IGET_NO_CHECKS patch was landed in commit v2_15_65-12-ga7239d80fc and included in the el9.4 series, so the patch should be part of the ldiskfs module.

It looks like this happened in the mount.lustre process rather than OI_Scrub in LU-18080. The test case is conf-sanity test_61b which was added by shadow via patch https://review.whamcloud.com/46486 "LU-15542 osd-ldiskfs: exclude EA inode from processing" which it case is deliberately linking an EA_INODE inode into lost+found?

The MDS console is spewing this message for more than an hour:

[Sat Aug 17 06:39:11 2024] LDISKFS-fs error (device dm-3): ldiskfs_lookup:2237: inode #161: comm mount.lustre: unexpected EA_INODE flag
[Sat Aug 17 06:39:11 2024] LDISKFS-fs error (device dm-3): ldiskfs_lookup:2237: inode #161: comm mount.lustre: unexpected EA_INODE flag
[Sat Aug 17 06:39:16 2024] LDISKFS-fs error: 43661 callbacks suppressed
[Sat Aug 17 06:39:16 2024] LDISKFS-fs error (device dm-3): ldiskfs_lookup:2237: inode #161: comm mount.lustre: unexpected EA_INODE flag
[Sat Aug 17 06:39:16 2024] LDISKFS-fs error (device dm-3): ldiskfs_lookup:2237: inode #161: comm mount.lustre: unexpected EA_INODE flag
[Sat Aug 17 06:39:16 2024] LDISKFS-fs error (device dm-3): ldiskfs_lookup:2237: inode #161: comm mount.lustre: unexpected EA_INODE flag

so I guess that mount.lustre is triggering the initial OI Scrub of the ldiskfs lost+found directory (osd_initial_OI_scrub() calling osd_ios_lf_fill()) and it is getting stuck forever in a loop? The stack trace looks like:

[Sat Aug 17 07:48:44 2024] task:mount.lustre    state:D stack:0     pid:696610 ppid:696608 flags:0x00004002
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:44 2024] Call Trace:
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:44 2024]  <TASK>
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:44 2024]  ? io_schedule+0x42/0x70
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:44 2024]  ? __wait_on_bit+0x12b/0x150
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:44 2024]  ? submit_bh_wbc+0x115/0x140
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:44 2024]  ? ldiskfs_commit_super+0xf9/0x150 [ldiskfs]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:44 2024]  ? ldiskfs_handle_error+0x1c0/0x1d0 [ldiskfs]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:44 2024]  ? __ldiskfs_error_inode+0x95/0x130 [ldiskfs]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:44 2024]  ? inode_init_always+0x1af/0x220
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:44 2024]  ? ldiskfs_alloc_inode+0x20/0x1c0 [ldiskfs]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:44 2024]  ? __d_alloc+0x29/0x1f0
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:44 2024]  ? __ldiskfs_iget+0xc0f/0xf00 [ldiskfs]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? ldiskfs_lookup.part.0+0x130/0x370 [ldiskfs]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? __lookup_slow+0x84/0x130
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? lookup_one_len+0x84/0x90
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? osd_lookup_one_len+0x18/0xc0 [osd_ldiskfs]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? do_osd_ios_lf_fill.constprop.0+0x119/0x640 [osd_ldiskfs]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? ldiskfs_readdir+0x20e/0x740 [ldiskfs]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? iterate_dir+0x17c/0x1c0
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? __pfx_osd_ios_lf_fill+0x10/0x10 [osd_ldiskfs]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? osd_ios_general_scan+0x113/0x270 [osd_ldiskfs]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? osd_initial_OI_scrub+0xd0/0x880 [osd_ldiskfs]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? osd_scrub_setup+0x85b/0x9b0 [osd_ldiskfs]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? osd_device_init0+0x533/0x8d0 [osd_ldiskfs]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? osd_device_alloc+0x19d/0x230 [osd_ldiskfs]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? obd_setup+0xaa/0x300 [obdclass]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? class_setup+0x5cb/0x800 [obdclass]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? class_process_config+0x173c/0x1bc0 [obdclass]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? do_lcfg+0x23a/0x530 [obdclass]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? lustre_start_simple+0x78/0x1e0 [obdclass]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? osd_start+0x459/0x690 [ptlrpc]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? lsi_prepare+0x2fd/0x4e0 [ptlrpc]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? server_fill_super+0x6e/0x790 [ptlrpc]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? lustre_fill_super+0x38e/0x480 [lustre]
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? mount_nodev+0x44/0x90
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? do_new_mount+0x17a/0x310
[Sat Aug 17 07:48:45 2024]  ? do_syscall_64+0x5c/0x90

yujian it looks like this is the same issue as LU-18080, where dongyang introduced a patch to fix this.

Is it possible that the server was missing ext4-add-IGET_NO_CHECKS-flag.patch in the ldiskfs patch series?

Console log on MDS:

[16755.993599] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: mkdir -p /mnt/lustre-mds1; mount -t lustre -o localrecov  /dev/mapper/mds1_flakey /mnt/lustre-mds1
[16756.197918] LDISKFS-fs (dm-3): mounted filesystem b585105d-4db5-4521-956d-9d74f55362e2 r/w with ordered data mode. Quota mode: journalled.
[16756.208248] LDISKFS-fs error (device dm-3): ldiskfs_lookup:2237: inode #161: comm mount.lustre: unexpected EA_INODE flag
[16756.208273] Aborting journal on device dm-3-8.
[16756.208598] LDISKFS-fs (dm-3): Remounting filesystem read-only
[16756.208631] LDISKFS-fs error (device dm-3): ldiskfs_lookup:2237: inode #161: comm mount.lustre: unexpected EA_INODE flag
